name: Conical Horn
description: A conical horn antenna is a circular waveguide that gradually flares outward in a conical shape to provide impedance matching between the waveguide and free space. It offers moderate gain with good circular symmetry and is commonly used as a feed for reflector antennas. The conical geometry provides uniform illumination with relatively low sidelobes.

aperture_diameter.name: Aperture Diameter
aperture_diameter.description: Physical diameter of the horn aperture opening

axial_length.name: Axial Length
axial_length.description: Distance from throat to aperture along the horn axis

throat_diameter.name: Throat Diameter
throat_diameter.description: Diameter of the circular waveguide at the horn throat

flare_angle.name: Flare Angle
flare_angle.description: Half-angle of the conical flare from the horn axis

slant_length.name: Slant Length
slant_length.description: Distance from the virtual apex to the aperture edge

designMethodology.overview: Conical horn design involves optimizing the aperture size and flare angle to achieve desired gain and beamwidth while maintaining good impedance matching and low sidelobes.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency
steps.description: Select the center frequency and bandwidth requirements

steps.title: Calculate Throat Diameter
steps.description: Size the circular waveguide throat for single-mode operation
steps.formula: d = 0.64 * lambda for TE11 mode cutoff

steps.title: Determine Aperture Diameter
steps.description: Calculate aperture size based on desired gain
steps.formula: D = sqrt(4 * A_eff / pi) where A_eff = G * lambda^2 / (4 * pi * eta)

steps.title: Optimize Axial Length
steps.description: Calculate length for optimal phase distribution
steps.formula: L_opt = D^2 / (8 * lambda) for minimum phase error

steps.title: Verify Impedance Matching
steps.description: Check VSWR across the operating bandwidth

equations.resonantFrequency: f_c = 1.841 * c / (pi * d) for TE11 mode cutoff
equations.gain: G = eta * (pi * D / lambda)^2 where eta ≈ 0.5 to 0.8

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line theory with gradual taper approximation and reflection coefficient calculation based on impedance mismatch
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply aperture integration method with uniform amplitude and quadratic phase distribution across circular aperture

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 12
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 11
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 15
